texto: Your children will be like olive shootsaround your table." -Psalm 128:3Children are likened to olive plants. Olive plants, if not pruned and controlled, become a wild nuisance. On the other hand, small olive plants that are nurtured and trained in the way they should grow do not grow wild and do not have scars from pruning since the pruning is done while they are young and tender. The later you do the training, the more scars they will have and the less likely there will will be success in directing their growth.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Joseph Stephen: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. O trecho “Your children will be like olive shootsaround your table." -Psalm 128:3Children are likened to ol…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que familia, bible, children pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de dia-chuvoso e termino.

frase_original: Your children will be like olive shootsaround your table." -Psalm 128:3Children are likened to olive plants. Olive plants, if not pruned and controlled, become a wild nuisance. On the other hand, small olive plants that are nurtured and trained in the way they should grow do not grow wild and do not have scars from pruning since the pruning is done while they are young and tender. The later you do the training, the more scars they will have and the less likely there will will be success in directing their growth.
